presentation.name: Hi, I'm
presentation.title: Senior Software Engineer
presentation.subtitle: I build frontend systems that hold up over time. My focus is Next.js, TypeScript, and making the architectural calls that keep teams from slowing down.

think.label: How I think
think.heading: The code is not the product. The solution is.
think.body: Messy code is not always a sign of bad decisions. Sometimes it was the right call at the time. I refactor when it starts slowing people down, not because it bothers me aesthetically, but when it is worth it and it gives the team more confidence to move.

decide.label: How I decide
decide.heading: I default to simple
decide.body: Complexity is a cost. I add it only when there is a clear reason for it, and I make sure that reason is documented. Most of the time, simple is enough.

ship.label: How I ship
ship.heading: I own what I put out
ship.body: The code has to be easy to read, understand, and change without having to find me first.

collaborate.label: How I collaborate
collaborate.heading: I want the people around me to do well
collaborate.body: I get more satisfaction from unblocking someone than from shipping something alone. A team that moves well is worth more than any individual output. Code is written by people for people, and clarity is what makes that work.

articles.title: Writing
articles.quote: I write when I've worked through something worth sharing. Practical pieces on architecture, tradeoffs, and problems I've run into in real codebases.

references.title: What people say
references.quote: Feedback from engineers and managers I've worked with, across different companies, timezones, and stages of product.
references.leave-reference: Worked with me? Leave a recommendation on LinkedIn.
